Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|12=draft|12=draft}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|12=draft|12=draft}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=draft|12=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|12=draft|12=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|12=draft|12=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|12=draft]] [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]]

'''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|de|Thronbesteigung im Alten Ägypten|ja|即位 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及登基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|de|Skorpion II.|ja|スコルピオン2世|zh|蝎子二世|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|賽德節|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|de|Narmer|ja|ナルメル|zh|納爾邁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|de|Pithom|ja|ピトム|zh|皮索姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]]

'''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|de|Thronbesteigung im Alten Ägypten|ja|即位 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及登基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|de|Skorpion II.|ja|スコルピオン2世|zh|蝎子二世|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|賽德節|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|de|Narmer|ja|ナルメル|zh|納爾邁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|de|Pithom|ja|ピトム|zh|皮索姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

'''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|de|Thronbesteigung im Alten Ägypten|ja|即位 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及登基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|de|Skorpion II.|ja|スコルピオン2世|zh|蝎子二世|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|賽德節|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|de|Narmer|ja|ナルメル|zh|納爾邁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|de|Pithom|ja|ピトム|zh|皮索姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}}

revision.text:
'''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|de|Thronbesteigung im Alten Ägypten|ja|即位 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及登基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|de|Skorpion II.|ja|スコルピオン2世|zh|蝎子二世|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|賽德節|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|de|Narmer|ja|ナルメル|zh|納爾邁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|de|Pithom|ja|ピトム|zh|皮索姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}}

'''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|de|Thronbesteigung im Alten Ägypten|ja|即位 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及登基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|de|Skorpion II.|ja|スコルピオン2世|zh|蝎子二世|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|賽德節|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|de|Narmer|ja|ナルメル|zh|納爾邁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|de|Pithom|ja|ピトム|zh|皮索姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages without German translation box]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}}

Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}}

revision.text:
'''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|de|Thronbesteigung im Alten Ägypten|ja|即位 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及登基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|de|Skorpion II.|ja|スコルピオン2世|zh|蝎子二世|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|賽德節|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|de|Narmer|ja|ナルメル|zh|納爾邁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|de|Pithom|ja|ピトム|zh|皮索姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn: failed]]

'''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|de|Thronbesteigung im Alten Ägypten|ja|即位 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及登基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|de|Skorpion II.|ja|スコルピオン2世|zh|蝎子二世|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|賽德節|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|de|Narmer|ja|ナルメル|zh|納爾邁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|de|Pithom|ja|ピトム|zh|皮索姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wgoodbye $1.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

revision.text:
'''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|de|Thronbesteigung im Alten Ägypten|ja|即位 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及登基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|de|Skorpion II.|ja|スコルピオン2世|zh|蝎子二世|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|賽德節|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|de|Narmer|ja|ナルメル|zh|納爾邁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|de|Pithom|ja|ピトム|zh|皮索姆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wgoodbye $1.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. revision.text:
'''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|de|Thronbesteigung im Alten Ägypten|ja|即位 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及登基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement in Ancient Egypt|qq=}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|de|Skorpion II.|ja|スコルピオン2世|zh|蝎子二世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scorpion II|qq=}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasty 0|qq=}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|de|Oberägypten|ja|上エジプト|zh|上埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Egypt|qq=}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|de|Unterägypten|ja|下エジプト|zh|下埃及|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Egypt|qq=}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|de|Jochem Kahl|ja|ヨヘム・カール|zh|約亨·卡爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jochem Kahl|qq=}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|qq=}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|de|Peter Funke|ja|ペーター・フンケ|zh|彼得·豐克|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peter Funke|qq=}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|賽德節|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|de|Narmer|ja|ナルメル|zh|納爾邁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narmer|qq=}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|qq=}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|de|Pithom|ja|ピトム|zh|皮索姆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pithom|qq=}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|de|Menes|ja|メネス|zh|美尼斯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menes|qq=}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|qq=}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|de|Chasechemui|ja|カセケムイ|zh|卡塞肯威|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khasekhemwy|qq=}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|ja|中王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及中王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|de|Hatschepsut|ja|ハトシェプスト|zh|哈特謝普蘇特|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatshepsut|qq=}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|理查德·安東尼·帕克|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wgoodbye $1.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the [[:en:Coronation of the pharaoh|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt]]''') is attested in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] since King [[:en:Scorpion II|Scorpion II]] in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasty 0|qq=}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of [[:en:Upper Egypt|Upper Egypt]] and [[:en:Lower Egypt|Lower Egypt]] did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.[[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|qq=}}, Anke Blöbaum, [[:en:Peter Funke|Peter Funke]] (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed festival]], as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. [[:en:Narmer|Narmer]] renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|qq=}}, which included the area of [[:en:Pithom|Pithom]] among others. Under [[:en:Menes|Menes]], who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|qq=}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was [[:en:Khasekhemwy|Khasekhemwy]], who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ([[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]]). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Coronations]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Https://de.wgoodbye $1.org/wiki/]][[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[sn:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|qq=}}, Anke Blöbaum, [[:en:Peter Funke|Peter Funke]] (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed festival]], as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. [[:en:Narmer|Narmer]] renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|qq=}}, which included the area of [[:en:Pithom|Pithom]] among others. Under [[:en:Menes|Menes]], who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|qq=}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was [[:en:Khasekhemwy|Khasekhemwy]], who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ([[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]]). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur.
