revision.text:
[[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the [[:en:Coronation of the pharaoh|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt]]''') is attested in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] since King [[:en:Scorpion II|Scorpion II]] in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasty 0|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of [[:en:Upper Egypt|Upper Egypt]] and [[:en:Lower Egypt|Lower Egypt]] did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.[[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Anke Blöbaum, [[:en:Peter Funke|Peter Funke]] (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed festival]], as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. [[:en:Narmer|Narmer]] renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which included the area of [[:en:Pithom|Pithom]] among others. Under [[:en:Menes|Menes]], who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was [[:en:Khasekhemwy|Khasekhemwy]], who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ([[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]]). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Coronation]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Coronation]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the [[:en:Coronation of the pharaoh|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt]]''') is attested in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] since King [[:en:Scorpion II|Scorpion II]] in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasty 0|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of [[:en:Upper Egypt|Upper Egypt]] and [[:en:Lower Egypt|Lower Egypt]] did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.[[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Anke Blöbaum, [[:en:Peter Funke|Peter Funke]] (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed festival]], as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. [[:en:Narmer|Narmer]] renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which included the area of [[:en:Pithom|Pithom]] among others. Under [[:en:Menes|Menes]], who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was [[:en:Khasekhemwy|Khasekhemwy]], who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ([[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]]). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Coronation]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Anke Blöbaum, [[:en:Peter Funke|Peter Funke]] (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed festival]], as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. [[:en:Narmer|Narmer]] renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which included the area of [[:en:Pithom|Pithom]] among others. Under [[:en:Menes|Menes]], who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was [[:en:Khasekhemwy|Khasekhemwy]], who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ([[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]]). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Coronation]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the [[:en:Coronation of the pharaoh|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt]]''') is attested in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] since King [[:en:Scorpion II|Scorpion II]] in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasty 0|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of [[:en:Upper Egypt|Upper Egypt]] and [[:en:Lower Egypt|Lower Egypt]] did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.[[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Anke Blöbaum, [[:en:Peter Funke|Peter Funke]] (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed festival]], as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. [[:en:Narmer|Narmer]] renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which included the area of [[:en:Pithom|Pithom]] among others. Under [[:en:Menes|Menes]], who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was [[:en:Khasekhemwy|Khasekhemwy]], who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ([[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]]). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Coronation]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the [[:en:Coronation of the pharaoh|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt]]''') is attested in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] since King [[:en:Scorpion II|Scorpion II]] in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasty 0|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of [[:en:Upper Egypt|Upper Egypt]] and [[:en:Lower Egypt|Lower Egypt]] did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.[[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Anke Blöbaum, [[:en:Peter Funke|Peter Funke]] (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed festival]], as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. [[:en:Narmer|Narmer]] renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which included the area of [[:en:Pithom|Pithom]] among others. Under [[:en:Menes|Menes]], who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was [[:en:Khasekhemwy|Khasekhemwy]], who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ([[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]]). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Coronation]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}}

Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Coronation]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

revision.text:
[[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the [[:en:Coronation of the pharaoh|Enthronement in Ancient Egypt]]''') is attested in [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] since King [[:en:Scorpion II|Scorpion II]] in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasty 0|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of [[:en:Upper Egypt|Upper Egypt]] and [[:en:Lower Egypt|Lower Egypt]] did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.[[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Anke Blöbaum, [[:en:Peter Funke|Peter Funke]] (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed festival]], as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. [[:en:Narmer|Narmer]] renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which included the area of [[:en:Pithom|Pithom]] among others. Under [[:en:Menes|Menes]], who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was [[:en:Khasekhemwy|Khasekhemwy]], who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ([[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]]). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Coronation]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

An exception in the Old Kingdom was [[:en:Khasekhemwy|Khasekhemwy]], who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ([[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]]). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Coronation]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

revision.text:
[[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Unification Festival |NAME = {{{!}} {{!}}W3 {{!}}[[File:Semataui.png|x46px]] {{!}}} Heb-sema-taui
''Ḥb-sm3-t3wj''
''Unification Festival of the Two Lands'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|ja|先王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|埃及前王朝時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |BILD1 = |BILD1-BREITE = |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = }} The '''Unification Festival''' (also '''Day of the Festival of the {{ill|Coronation of the pharaoh|en|Coronation of the pharaoh|WD=Q2429635|lt=Enthronement in Ancient Egypt}}''') is attested in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} since King {{ill|Scorpion II|en|Scorpion II|WD=Q219809|lt=Scorpion II}} in the {{ill|Dynasty 0|de|0. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasty 0|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|en|Upper Egypt|WD=Q203751|lt=Upper Egypt}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|en|Lower Egypt|WD=Q463871|lt=Lower Egypt}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|en|Jochem Kahl|WD=Q1691027|lt=Jochem Kahl}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|en|Peter Funke|WD=Q1395093|lt=Peter Funke}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|WD=Q604644|lt=Sed festival}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|en|Narmer|WD=Q189582|lt=Narmer}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|en|Pithom|WD=Q1630019|lt=Pithom}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|en|Menes|WD=Q189574|lt=Menes}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|en|Khasekhemwy|WD=Q294266|lt=Khasekhemwy}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Coronation]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Dynastie|ja|エジプト第0王朝|zh|埃及第零王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasty 0|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Predynastic Period). However, since its first attestation, it held only symbolic character. Actual governance over the parts of {{ill|Upper Egypt|en|Upper Egypt|WD=Q203751|lt=Upper Egypt|1=Upper Egypt}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|en|Lower Egypt|WD=Q463871|lt=Lower Egypt|1=Lower Egypt}} did not originally exist, especially as during the predynastic period, the "two lands" were geographically defined differently.{{ill|Jochem Kahl|en|Jochem Kahl|WD=Q1691027|lt=Jochem Kahl|1=Jochem Kahl}}: ''Ober- und Unterägypten. Eine dualistische Konstruktion und ihre Anfänge.'' In: {{ill|Rainer Albertz|de|Rainer Albertz|ja|ライナー・アルベーツ|zh|賴納·阿爾貝茨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Albertz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Anke Blöbaum, {{ill|Peter Funke|en|Peter Funke|WD=Q1395093|lt=Peter Funke|1=Peter Funke}} (Eds.): ''Räume und Grenzen. Topologische Konzepte in den antiken Kulturen des östlichen Mittelmeerraums'' (= ''Quellen und Forschungen zur antiken Welt.'' Volume 52). Utz, München 2007, ISBN 978-3-8316-0699-3, p. 3–28, here p. 13 ([http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2017/3545 online]). == Background == In the course of ancient Egyptian history, kings also celebrated the Unification Festival as a jubilee festival during extended reigns, conducted for the renewal of rule. Therefore, the Unification Festival can be considered a possible precursor to the {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|WD=Q604644|lt=Sed festival|1=Sed festival}}, as later the celebration of the Unification Festival no longer appeared as an anniversary under this designation. === Early Period and Old Kingdom === Under Scorpion II, a violent unification of Lower and Upper Egypt was first evidenced, with his capital destroyed during the decisive conquest campaign. {{ill|Narmer|en|Narmer|WD=Q189582|lt=Narmer|1=Narmer}} renewed the "forced unification" by conquering the {{ill|Harpoon nome|de|Harpunengau|ja|銛州|zh|魚叉諾姆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harpoon nome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which included the area of {{ill|Pithom|en|Pithom|WD=Q1630019|lt=Pithom|1=Pithom}} among others. Under {{ill|Menes|en|Menes|WD=Q189574|lt=Menes|1=Menes}}, who achieved a peaceful and equal {{ill|Egyptian unification|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの統一|zh|埃及統一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian unification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the meaning of the festival changed; thereafter, it symbolized the shared and peaceful rule over Lower and Upper Egypt. Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|en|Khasekhemwy|WD=Q294266|lt=Khasekhemwy|1=Khasekhemwy}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz 1950. == References == ==References== {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|version=240032978|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] }} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes %C3%84gypten)]] [[ja:上下エジプト統一祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[de:Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[en:Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] {{wikidata link|Q2515140}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Coronation]] [[Category:Kingship (Egypt)]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

Inscriptions and depictions on monuments of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代|zh|古王國時期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show that from the first unification, the Unification Festival was celebrated together with the Enthronement Festival. An exception in the Old Kingdom was {{ill|Khasekhemwy|en|Khasekhemwy|WD=Q294266|lt=Khasekhemwy|1=Khasekhemwy}}, who did not celebrate the Unification Festival upon accession and only did so after the renewed forced subjugation of Lower Egypt. === The Unification Festival from the Middle Kingdom Onwards === From the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, the Unification Festival is regularly documented, though without assignment to a specific date, as it emphasizes the significance of the kingdom's unification by Menes with inscriptions such as "Proclamation of Peace" and "End of Strife." Due to the merging of the festivals "Day of the King's Coronation" and "Unification of the Two Lands," the name of the Unification Festival later appears only rarely as a documentary annotation, for instance when contrary to tradition, a woman proclaimed herself ruler with the king's title ({{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). Otherwise, the "Unification of the Two Lands" automatically became part of the "Day of the King's Coronation." == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|齊格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Volume 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}).
